seoTitle: How to Insert a PNG Image in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to upload, resize, and position transparent PNG images in Canva to create polished logos and marketing graphics.
intro: PNG images with transparent backgrounds open up more design flexibility, especially for logos and layered graphics. This tutorial covers uploading a PNG into Canva, resizing it, and positioning it correctly within your design.

steps.heading: Understand PNG images
steps.body: Learn what makes PNG files useful, particularly their support for transparency.

steps.heading: Upload PNGs in Canva
steps.body: Bring your PNG image files into your Canva project.

steps.heading: Resize and position your image
steps.body: Adjust the size and placement of the PNG so it fits naturally within your design.

steps.heading: Follow final tips and best practices
steps.body: Apply tips for using PNGs effectively across logos, promotional content, and branding.

faq.question: Why use a PNG instead of another image format?
faq.answer: PNG files support transparent backgrounds, which is useful for logos and layered design work.

faq.question: Can this help with branding and marketing materials?
faq.answer: Yes, transparent PNGs make it easier to create eye-catching logos and unique promotional content for a brand.

faq.question: What common mistakes should I avoid?
faq.answer: Common mistakes include using low-resolution PNGs or incorrect sizing that distorts the image once placed.

faq.question: Does Canva support AI-driven design alongside PNG uploads?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva's AI-driven design features can further enhance projects that include uploaded PNG images.
